items.headline: City Reports on Children and Youth Services Master Plan
items.summary: San José City Council received a status report on the Children and Youth Services Master Plan on April 9, including updates on the Youth Empowerment Alliance, Safe Summer Initiative Grant, and youth intervention programs. This indicates the city is evaluating and planning its youth services offerings.

items.headline: Zoning hearing for 333 Franklin Street postponed
items.summary: A scheduled administrative zoning hearing for a project at 333 Franklin Street has been cancelled and rescheduled for April 22, 2026. The project will be re-noticed once a new hearing date is confirmed.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project Moves Forward
items.summary: City Council approved the third amendment to the consultant agreement for the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project on April 7. This infrastructure project addresses traffic and safety at the intersection of highway and rail in San José.

items.headline: City updates Active Transportation Plan with revised scoring criteria
items.summary: Cupertino is advancing Phase 2 of its Active Transportation Plan, which will guide bike lanes, pedestrian pathways, and transit improvements across the city. The revised scoring criteria will determine which projects get built first, directly affecting how residents can get around without cars.

items.headline: Senior housing complex with 65 units approved on East St John
items.summary: Trillium senior apartments will bring nearly 9 million dollars of investment and 65 units of much-needed senior housing to East St John Street. This is a significant addition to San José's senior living options in an established neighborhood.

items.headline: City Council Approves Emergency Interim Housing Siting Policy
items.summary: San José City Council adopted a new Emergency Interim Housing Siting Policy on April 1, which will affect where the city can place temporary housing for homeless residents. This policy change could impact neighborhood planning and emergency response to homelessness across the city.

items.headline: Garden Gate Coalition sues city over Mary Avenue safety
items.summary: An ongoing legal case, Garden Gate Coalition for Mary Avenue Safety v. City of Cupertino, is in Superior Court. The case suggests a neighborhood group has concerns about safety conditions on Mary Avenue that the city and residents should be aware of.

items.headline: City Council approves changes to natural gas rates for 2027
items.summary: Palo Alto is adjusting its residential and commercial natural gas service rates for the fiscal year starting July 2026. Residents should review the rate changes for G-1 (residential), G-2 (commercial), and G-3 (large commercial) service to understand how their utility bills may be affected.
